Home Tasks Leaderboard

GenAI Games — Tasks & Submission Rules

Here you will find information about the challenges, data downloads, and submission guidelines for the GenAI Games.

General Submission Rules for All Tasks

Each team submission must include:

  • Team name
  • Main output (the solution/deliverable)
  • How we did it — This is important! The goal is not only to solve the task, but also to learn how different AI approaches work
    • AI tools used — Which tools did you employ?
    • Main prompt or workflow — What did you ask the AI to do?
    • What we checked manually — Did you verify the AI output?
    • Where AI helped most — Which part of the task did AI solve best?
    • Confidence level: 1–5 — How confident are you in your solution?

8 Challenges

Task 1: Bumbling Baboon

Type: Presentation / Communication
Create a 5-minute presentation on another group's research topic using AI.

View & Submit

Task 2: Picasso, Is That You?

Type: Poster / Visual Communication
Create an A1 research poster introducing a Ghent research domain.

View & Submit

Task 3: Into the Blue

Type: Research / Literature Mapping
Produce a one-page literature scan on a narrowly scoped research question.

View & Submit

Task 4: Just Give Me a Moment

Type: Technical Reasoning (Steel)
Solve a structural steel frame problem using AI support.

View & Submit

Task 5: Time to Start Cracking

Type: Technical Reasoning (Concrete)
Solve a cement hydration problem using AI reasoning.

View & Submit

Task 6: Good Vibes

Type: Technical Reasoning (Dynamics)
Solve a footbridge vibration problem with TMD design.

View & Submit

Task 7: And Then There Was Light

Type: Coding + Visualization
Compute daylight metrics and create a pass/fail floor-plan visualization.

View & Submit

Task 8: CHOO CHOO

Type: Advanced Coding + Depth
Extract railway gauge values and visualize tolerance compliance.

View & Submit
